what_it_does: Defines a React left navigation sidebar with a ConceptV wordmark, fixed navigation item groups, collapsible sections for Tools, Integrations, and Support, active item styling, and selection callbacks for main and tool items.
observations: Reads useState from the global React object instead of importing React or hooks.
Stores top-level navigation and tool entries as local constant arrays with id, emoji glyph, and label fields.
Section manages its own open/closed state and renders children only when open.
PlatformSidebar renders a logo, Menu divider label, main nav buttons, and three section groups.
Main nav and tool buttons call onSelect with their item id and apply an active class when active matches the id.
Integrations and Support buttons are static buttons without onSelect wiring or active-state handling.
The file assigns PlatformSidebar to window.PlatformSidebar.
standouts: The sidebar combines always-visible primary navigation with locally collapsible secondary groups.
Emoji glyphs are used as the visual icon system for all visible navigation actions.
Placeholder slots communicate where favourited or activated options would appear.
conventions: Uses local data arrays to render repeated navigation buttons.
Uses className hooks for most styling while also applying several inline style objects.
Uses a global component export pattern through window assignment.
Uses conditional class names with template literals for active state.
concerns: Inline styles are mixed with class-based styling, which can make visual rules split across JSX and CSS.
Emoji glyphs may render inconsistently across platforms and may need accessibility handling outside this file.
Integrations and Support buttons look interactive but do not trigger selection callbacks in this file.
The component assumes onSelect is callable when main or tool nav items are clicked.
Placeholder copy is repeated and hard-coded.
notes: This file is a compact global React sidebar implementation with fixed menu data, collapsible groups, and placeholder extension areas.
questions: Should Integrations and Support items select views like the main and tool items?
Are the placeholder slots meant to be populated from props or external state?
Should the logo path and navigation item lists remain hard-coded in this component?
Is the sidebar expected to provide accessible labels beyond visible text and emoji glyphs?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose, "left nav rail", matches the rendered sidebar navigation rail with grouped menu actions.
intention_signals: It seems intended to provide a reusable ConceptV platform sidebar shell with recognizable navigation categories, collapsible secondary areas, and simple active-view selection.
novelty: The combination of emoji-based glyphs, hard-coded product-specific menu groups, and placeholder slots gives the sidebar a prototype or handoff-oriented character.
contribution: Contributes a file-local pattern for a branded left navigation rail with data-driven menu items, collapsible grouped sections, active-state classes, and reserved areas for future dynamic options.
summary_for_embedding: A React JSX file defining a ConceptV platform sidebar with logo branding, fixed navigation arrays, collapsible section groups, active item styling, and global window exposure.
intention_for_embedding: This was for presenting or wiring a left-side platform navigation shell with grouped tools, integrations, support links, and selectable views.

symbols.does: Renders a collapsible section with a title button, chevron indicator, and conditional child content.
symbols.description: Section is a small stateful React component that tracks whether its content is open using useState. It defaults to open unless defaultOpen is set otherwise, toggles when the section title button is clicked, and lays out children in a vertical flex column when expanded.

symbols.does: Renders the full ConceptV sidebar with brand mark, primary nav, tool nav, integration placeholders, and support links.
symbols.description: PlatformSidebar is the main exported component for this file. It takes active and onSelect props, uses active to apply selected styling to main and tool items, calls onSelect for those items, and composes multiple Section instances for secondary navigation groups.
